response.justification: Bogdan has a strong background in full-stack web development and some tangential geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, but fundamentally lacks the core technical requirements for this role. The position demands modern C++ or Rust as the primary language for systems-level routing pipeline work; his C++ exposure is minimal, dated (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015), and peripheral to the role. His geospatial work centers on data collection and visualization platforms, not routing tile generation or navigation guidance pipelines. There is no evidence of work with routing algorithms, map tile optimization, traffic signal integration, or production navigation systems.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain familiarity through GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk on open geo-data platforms
Proven high-throughput, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and performance-focused design
Broad distributed-systems exposure: microservices, Kubernetes, event-driven architectures, and CI/CD pipelines across multiple roles
concerns: Primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — no meaningful C++ or Rust experience; C++ is limited to a single role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project
Geospatial experience is scoped to data collection and mapping platforms for NGOs, with no exposure to routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance, or map data transformation for production routing
No demonstrated experience with routing algorithms, tile optimization, traffic signal integration, or the low-level OS/systems programming that this role requires
